LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYDS, LONDON, SUBSCRIBING TO POLICY NUMBER B0146LDUSA1304372 MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 425.16 IS GRANTED. THE FIRST PRONG WAS MET AS CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S BAD FAITH CLAIM ARISES OUT OF CROSS-DEFENDANT'S RIGHT OF PETITION SPECIFICALLY IT SEEKS A JUDICIAL DETERMINATION OF PARTIES RESPECTIVE RIGHTS UNDER AN INSURANCE POLICY. A DEFENDANT IS PRECLUDED FROM USING THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE TO STRIKE A CAUSE OF ACTION IF THE DEFENDANT CONCEDES, OR THE EVIDENCE CONCLUSIVELY ESTABLISHES THAT THE SPEECH OR PETITION ACTIVITY WAS ILLEGAL. THERE IS NO SUCH EVIDENCE HERE. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE NOT DEMONSTRATED PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE THE CROSS-DEFENDANT WITHHELD BENEFITS UNDER THE POLICY NOR WAS THERE EVIDENCE THAT BENEFITS WERE WITHHELD. ADDITIONALLY, THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE BARS THE CLAIM GIVEN THAT THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL ACT GIVEN RISE TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS THE FILING OF THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302.

Notice Of Hearing On Cross-Defendants Certain Underwriters At Lloyds, London, Subscribing To Policy Number B0146ldusa1304372'S Demurrer To Mcgrane Llp And William Mcgrane'S First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-23-2015 Advanced From Oct-26-15 To May-13-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Continued To Nov-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Certain Underwriters At Lloyds, London, Subscribing To Policy Number B0146ldusa1304372 Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike First Amended Cross-Complaint Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Â§ 425.16; Request For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar May-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew G. Wanger, of Clyde & Co. US LLP, Tel. 415.365.9800, for plaintiffs;
Jonathan R. Bass, of Coblentz Patch Duffy & Bass LLP, Tel. 415.391.4800, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS Certain Underwriters At Lloyds, London, Subscribing To Policy Number B0146ldusa1304372'S Special Motion To Strike First Amended Cross-Complaint Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sec. 425.16 is granted. The first prong was met as cross-complainant's bad faith claim arises out of cross-defendant's right of petition specifically it seeks a judicial determination of parties respective rights under an insurance policy. A defendant is precluded from using the Anti-Slapp statute to strike a cause of action if the defendant concedes, or the evidence conclusively establishes that the speech or petition activity was illegal. There is no such evidence here. Cross-complainants have not demonstrated probability of prevailing on the merits. The cross-complaint does not allege the cross-defendant withheld benefits under the policy nor was there evidence that benefits were withheld. Additionally, the litigation privilege bars the claim given that the alleged wrongful act given rise to the cross-complaint is the filing of the underlying complaint. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Hearing On Cross-Defendants Certain Underwriters At Lloyds, London, Subscribing To Policy Number B0146ldusa1304372'S Demurrer To Mcgrane Llp And William Mcgrane'S First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar May-15-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew G. Wanger, of Clyde & Co. US LLP, Tel. 415.365.9800, for plaintiffs;
Jonathan R. Bass, of Coblentz Patch Duffy & Bass LLP, Tel. 415.391.4800, for defendants.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court rules as follows: Cross-Defendants Certain Underwriters At Lloyds, London, Subscribing To Policy Number B0146ldusa1304372's Demurrer To McGrane LLP And William McGrane's First Amended Cross-Complaint is denied as moot because of the ruling on the Motion to Strike. Objections are sustained as to materials filed. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.
